steps.title: Substitute x = 0 to find the initial value
steps.content:
The equation $y = 390 - 142x$ gives remaining distance $y$ (in meters) and time $x$ (in hours). At the start of the experiment, $x = 0$.

Substituting:
$$y = 390 - 142(0) = 390$$
The remaining distance at the start is 390 meters.

steps.title: Identify 390 as the y-intercept and interpret it
steps.content:
In a linear equation $y = mx + b$, the constant term $b$ is the y-intercept—the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Here, 390 is the y-intercept.

The coefficient −142 represents the rate of change: the rover covers 142 meters per hour. So 142 is the rover's speed, not 390.

Since 390 is the remaining distance at the start of the experiment, it represents the rover's initial distance from its target. That matches choice C — C is correct.
